heading: Qualified overtime compensation
label: 225 Qualified overtime compensation
notes: References in TextSection 7 of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938, referred to in subsec. (c)(1), is classified to , Labor.section 207 of Title 29
Prior ProvisionsA prior section 225 was renumbered .section 226 of this title

Effective DateSection applicable to taxable years beginning after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2025 Amendment note under .Dec. 31, 2024section 70202(g) of Pub. L. 119–21section 63 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection applicable to taxable years beginning after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2025 Amendment note under .Dec. 31, 2024section 70202(g) of Pub. L. 119–21section 63 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: There shall be allowed as a deduction an amount equal to the qualified overtime compensation received during the taxable year and included on statements furnished to the individual pursuant to section 6041(d)(4) or 6051(a)(19).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount allowed as a deduction under this section for any taxable year shall not exceed $12,500 ($25,000 in the case of a joint return).

children.heading: Limitation based on adjusted gross income
children.label: 2 Limitation based on adjusted gross income

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The amount allowable as a deduction under subsection (a) (after application of paragraph (1)) shall be reduced (but not below zero) by $100 for each $1,000 by which the taxpayer’s modified adjusted gross income exceeds $150,000 ($300,000 in the case of a joint return).

children.heading: Modified adjusted gross income
children.label: B Modified adjusted gross income
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “modified adjusted gross income” means the adjusted gross income of the taxpayer for the taxable year increased by any amount excluded from gross income under section 911, 931, or 933.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “qualified overtime compensation” means overtime compensation paid to an individual required under section 7 of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 that is in excess of the regular rate (as used in such section) at which such individual is employed.

children.heading: Exclusions
children.label: 2 Exclusions
children.text: Such term shall not include any qualified tip (as defined in section 224(d)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this section unless the taxpayer includes on the return of tax for the taxable year such individual’s social security number.

children.heading: Social security number defined
children.label: 2 Social security number defined
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “social security number” shall have the meaning given such term in section 24(h)(7).

children.heading: Married individuals
children.label: e Married individuals
children.text: If the taxpayer is a married individual (within the meaning of section 7703), this section shall apply only if the taxpayer and the taxpayer’s spouse file a joint return for the taxable year.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: f Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall issue such regulations or other guidance as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section, including regulations or other guidance to prevent abuse of the deduction allowed by this section.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: g Termination
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this section for any taxable year beginning after .December 31, 2028
